name: The Hassan Foundation
mission: TO PROMOTE HUMAN DEVELOPMENT BY PRESERVING LIFE THROUGH IMPROVING HEALTH AND EDUCATION AMONGST THE POOR PEOPLE IN PAKISTAN. TO PROVIDE CLEAN POTABLE WAT...
theoryOfChange: Provision of clean potable water to underserved communities to reduce illness and deaths from contaminated water and to reduce the burden of traveling long distances for water; support for education and vocational training as additional longer-term support.
scoreSummary: The Hassan Foundation shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 100 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions high financial efficiency and direct service model as strengths, but it doesn't adequately address the significant deduction due to the excessive working capital (99.7 months), which is a key factor in tempering the overall score. The rationale should also mention the risk deduction due to the zakat compliance issue.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The foundation shows high alignment for Muslim donors as it is Zakat-eligible and focuses on high-priority religious and cultural causes in Pakistan [4]. Its long-term sustainability is supported by a robust working capital of 99.7 months [3].

While it provides direct services like safe water and education, the low data confidence score reflects a lack of extensive third-party evidence regarding specific outcomes [3].

impact.improvement: To improve impact, the charity should <cite id="15">publish a plan to deploy its excess reserves</cite> into new projects rather than holding 8 years of cash.

strengths.detail: The charity directs <cite id="4">88.2% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core mission programs in Pakistan.
The foundation maintains a <cite id="6">$0.00 fundraising efficiency ratio</cite>, meaning no donor funds are spent on professional solicitation.
With <cite id="10">99.7 months of working capital</cite>, the organization has extreme long-term sustainability and resilience against funding fluctuations.

strengths_deep_dive: The Hassan Foundation's financial model is characterized by extreme leaness and high directness. By maintaining a <cite id="4">88.2% program expense ratio</cite>, the organization ensures that nearly every dollar contributed supports its <cite id="2">clean water and education initiatives</cite>. This is supported by a governance structure where <cite id="12">100% of the board members are independent</cite>, providing oversight without financial conflict. Furthermore, the charity's ability to operate with <cite id="6">zero fundraising expenses</cite> allows it to maximize the impact of small-to-medium donor contributions compared to larger organizations with heavy marketing budgets.
rich_narrative.summary: The Hassan Foundation is a dedicated humanitarian organization that focuses on preserving life and promoting human development in Pakistan. Since its founding in <cite id="1">2007</cite>, the charity has specialized in <cite id="2">clean water projects and education support</cite> for underserved populations. The organization operates with a lean financial model, reporting <cite id="3">$586,737 in total revenue</cite> for the 2022 fiscal year. A defining feature of their operations is the high level of direct funding. They maintain a <cite id="4">88.2% program expense ratio</cite>, which significantly exceeds the <cite id="5">75% industry standard</cite> for non-profits. This efficiency is further bolstered by their fundraising strategy. The charity spends <cite id="6">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in donations, ensuring that public contributions are not consumed by marketing costs. Beyond water and schooling, the foundation provides <cite id="7">orphan support and disaster relief</cite> to communities facing acute crises. As a <cite id="8">Zakat-eligible organization</cite>, it specifically serves the <cite id="9">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> categories. While the foundation demonstrates strong financial health, it maintains an unusually high level of liquidity. It currently holds <cite id="10">99.7 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. This large reserve ensures long-term stability but suggests a significant opportunity to expand immediate program delivery. The charity's work is primarily implemented through <cite id="11">foreign grants totaling $712,272</cite> to local partners in Pakistan, facilitating direct service in hard-to-reach regions.
